subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution: Fluorine is present mainly as insoluble fluorides (fluorspar CaF2, cryolite Na3AlF6 and fluoroapatite 3Ca3(PO4 )2 .CaF2 ). Sea water contains chlorides, bromides and iodides of sodium, potassium, magnesium and calcium, but is mainly sodium chloride solution (2.5% by mass). The deposits of dried up seas contain these compounds, e.g., sodium chloride and carnallite, KCl.MgCl2 .6H2O.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
options: Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
solution:
In Electrolytic refining, the pure metal is used as cathode and impure metal is used as anode. Electrolytic refining of aluminium is called Hall-Heroult process. 

Na3AlF6 is added during electrolysis of Al2O3 to lower the melting point and increase conductivity.
Reactions at cathode and anode are as follows:
Cathode: Al3+(melt) + 3e- → Al(l)

Anode: C(s) + O2- (melt) → CO(g) + 2e-C(s) + 2O2- (melt) → CO2  (g) + 4e-

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution: Froth flotation is a process for selectively separating hydrophobic materials from hydrophilic. The principle of froth flotation process is that sulphide ores are preferentially wetted by the pine oil, whereas the gangue particles are wetted by the water. Cresols and aniline are used as froth stabilisers.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
options: Statement I is false but Statement II is true
Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Both Statenent I and Statement II are false
solution:
Ellingham diagram was a curve that related the Gibbs energy value with the temperature. Ellingham diagram is used to check which metal oxide is reduced by which compound.

ΔG=ΔH−TΔS

The slope of the curve is -∆S

Since the slope of the curve is positive, thus the ∆S is negative. So the value of ∆S decreases left to right.

solution:
According to the Ellingham diagram, the species that is going to act as reducing agent should lie below the substance to be reduced at the particular temperature.

 As is visible from the graph, at 1400o C, the line of Al lies below the line of ZnO. Hence, Al can be used for reducing ZnO to Zn.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are true.
Both Statement I and Statement II are false.
Statement I is true but Statement II is false.
Statement I is false but Statement II is true.
solution:
Impure Cu obtained from ores is converted to pure Cu in an electrolyte cell that the impure copper as the anode an pure copper as the cathode. The electrolyte is an aqueous solution of CuSO4. At the impure Cu anode, Cu is oxidized along with the more easily oxidized metallic impurities such as Zn and Fe. The less easily oxidized impurities such as Ag,Au, and Pt fall to the bottom of the cell as anode mud, which is reprocessed to recover the precious metals. At the pure Cu cathode, Cu2+ ions get reduced to pure copper metal

Anode (oxidation) : Ms⟶M2+aq+2e-M=Cu,Zn,Fe

Cathode (reduction) : Cu2+aq+2e-⟶Cus

Thus, the net cell reaction simply involves transfer of Cu metal from the impure anode to the pure cathode, Cu obtained by this process is 99.95% pure.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
For ZnS,KCN is used as depressant.

In the metallurgy of silver and gold, the respective metal is leached with a dilute solution of NaCN or KCN in the presence of air, which supplies O2. The metal is obtained later by replacement reaction.

4Ms+8CN-aq+2H2Oaq+O2g→4MCN2-aq+4OH-aq M=Ag or Au

2MCN2-aq+Zns→ZnCN42-aq+2Ms

Copper metal is not involved in this process.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
In the extraction of copper from its sulphide ore, metal is finally obtained by the oxidation of cuprous sulphide with Cu2O. ​ It is an example of auto reduction. 

Cu2S+2Cu2O →6Cu+ SO2

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
Liquation method is used when the melting point of metal is less compare to the melting point of the associated impurity.

In liquation process, the impure metal is heated on slopy hearth of furnace, due to less melting point, metal melts first and flow down leaving impurities behind.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution: Ellingham diagram is a graph showing temperature dependence of the stability of compounds. This analysis is used to evaluate the case of Reduction of metal oxides and sulphides. This diagram also used to predict the equilibrium temperature between a metal and its oxide.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
Mond's process involves the combination of carbon monoxide with nickel readily and reversibly and gives nickel tetracarbonyl. This process is used to extract and purify nickel.

The Van Arkel technique is used to refine Zirconium or Titanium. Impurities found in Zirconium or Titanium in the form of nitrogen and oxygen are eliminated using this technique.

Copper is purified by electrolysis. Electricity is passed through solutions containing copper compounds, such as copper sulphate. The anode (positive electrode ) is made from impure copper and the cathode (negative electrode) is made from pure copper.

Mercury and zinc are purified by distillation method. Liquation is a metallurgical method to remove lead containing silver from copper and remove antimony minerals from ore.

Calamine is the carbonate ore of zinc.It composition is ZnCO3.

Galena is also called lead glance is the natural mineral form of lead (ii) sulfide,PbS

Sphalerite is a sulfide mineral with the formula ZnS. It is the most important ore of zinc.

Siderite is a mineral composed of iron(II) carbonate (FeCO3).

Siderite is a mineral composed of iron (II) carbonate FeCO3. It is a widespread mineral that is an ore of iron. 

Calamine: Zinc ore is most commonly found as zinc carbonate ZnCO3 known as calamine or smithsonite. 

Malachite - CuCO3·Cu(OH)2,  Malachite is a copper carbonate hydroxide mineral

Cryolite - Na3AlF6, which consists of Aluminium, Sodium & Fluorine.

options: Statement I is true but Statement II is false.

solution:
Sphalerite is ZnS

Copper glance is Cu2S

It is possible to separate two sulphide ores by adjusting proportion of oil to water or by using 'depressants'. For example, in case of an ore containing ZnS and PbS, the depressant used is NaCN. It selectively prevents ZnS from coming to the froth but allows PbS to come with the froth.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
During extraction of copper, iron oxide present as impurity is removed by adding SiO2 and converting it into FeSiO3

FeO+SiO2→FeSiO3          Flux          Slag

Hence,FeO is gangue and FeSiO3 is slag.

solution: The copper(I) sulphide produced is converted to copper with a final blast of air. The end product of this is called blister copper - a porous brittle form of copper, about <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>98</mn><mo> </mo><mo>-</mo><mo> </mo><mn>99</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>%</mo></math> pure. The reactions given are carried out in the production of blister copper using self reduction.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution:
Mond process for metal refining: In this process, nickel is heated in a stream of carbon monoxide forming a volatile complex, nickel tetracarbonyl:

Ni + 4CO →330 - 350K Ni(CO)4

The carbonyl is subjected to a higher temperature so that it is decomposed giving the pure metal:

Ni(CO)4 →450 - 470K Ni + 4CO

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
solution: Froth stabilizers cresols, aniline are added to stabilize the froth and enhance the nonwettability of the mineral particles.
